heading: Solid Start program
label: 6320 Solid Start program
notes: Amendments2025—Subsec. (a)(2)(A). inserted “, including how to enroll in the system of annual patient enrollment established and operated under and the ability to seek care and services under sections 1703 and 1710 of this title” before “; and”.Pub. L. 118–210section 1705 of this title
amendments: Amendments2025—Subsec. (a)(2)(A). inserted “, including how to enroll in the system of annual patient enrollment established and operated under and the ability to seek care and services under sections 1703 and 1710 of this title” before “; and”.Pub. L. 118–210section 1705 of this title

children.text: build the capacity of the Department to efficiently and effectively respond to the queries and needs of veterans who have recently separated from the Armed Forces; and

children.label: A
children.text: to proactively reach out to newly separated veterans to inform them of their eligibility for programs of and benefits provided by the Department, including how to enroll in the system of annual patient enrollment established and operated under and the ability to seek care and services under sections 1703 and 1710 of this title; andsection 1705 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: to connect veterans in crisis to resources that address their immediate needs.

children.heading: .—Activities of the Solid Start Program
children.label: b .—Activities of the Solid Start Program
A
children.text: collecting up-to-date contact information during transition classes or separation counseling for all members of the Armed Forces who are separating from the Armed Forces, while explaining the existence and purpose of the Solid Start program;

children.label: B
children.text: calling each veteran, regardless of separation type or characterization of service, three times within the first year after separation of the veteran from the Armed Forces;

children.label: C
children.text: providing information about the Solid Start program on the website of the Department and in materials of the Department, especially transition booklets and other resources;

children.label: D
children.text: ensuring calls are truly tailored to the needs of each veteran’s unique situation by conducting quality assurance tests;

children.label: E
children.text: prioritizing outreach to veterans who have accessed mental health resources prior to separation from the Armed Forces;

children.label: F
children.text: providing women veterans with information that is tailored to their specific health care and benefit needs;

children.label: G
children.text: as feasible, providing information on access to State and local resources, including Vet Centers and veterans service organizations; and

children.label: H
children.text: gathering and analyzing data assessing the effectiveness of the Solid Start program.

children.label: A
children.text: encouraging members of the Armed Forces who are transitioning to civilian life to authorize alternate points of contact who can be reached should the member be unavailable during the first year following the separation of the member from the Armed Forces; and

children.label: B
children.text: following up missed phone calls with tailored mailings to ensure the veteran still receives similar information.

children.label: A
children.text: The term “Vet Center” has the meaning given that term in .section 1712A(h) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: The term “veterans service organization” means an organization recognized by the Secretary for the representation of veterans under .section 5902 of this title
